assumptions: The question is assessed at the level of the consolidated financial statements.

The intragroup dividend has created a recognised foreign-currency monetary receivable/payable between group entities with different functional currencies, so exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
The general designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements in IFRS 9 paragraph 6.4.1 are otherwise met.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of the intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 permits the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to be a hedged item in consolidated financial statements, and a recognised asset or liability exposed to a particular risk can be designated in a fair value hedge.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of the foreign-currency exposure
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct hedge route for variability in cash flows associated with a recognised asset or liability that could affect profit or loss.
